summary: The page should lead with a cash-flow clarity promise, show proof before feature depth, handle setup and trust objections, and repeat one demo-request action without competing CTAs.

findings.label: Promise must precede product detail
findings.evidence: The brief identifies founders and operators who need cash-flow visibility before investor or board updates.
findings.interpretation: The hero should orient around the board-update outcome before explaining features.
findings.handoff: Use create-wireframe to keep the hero focused on one demo-request action.

findings.label: Proof belongs before feature depth
findings.evidence: The required content asks for customer count, reporting time saved, and finance team trust before feature explanation.
findings.interpretation: Visitors need confidence before reading product mechanics.
findings.handoff: Place proof cards immediately after the hero before the feature section.

findings.label: Objections are conversion-critical
findings.evidence: The brief names setup time, accounting-system compatibility, and data accuracy as required objection handling.
findings.interpretation: The wireframe needs an objection section before the final demo CTA.
findings.handoff: Represent objections as comparison or FAQ-style support before final commitment.
